sanskrit: एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः । कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम् ॥
hindiTranslation: पूर्वकालके मुमुक्षुओंने भी इस प्रकार जानकर कर्म किये हैं, इसलिये तू भी पूर्वजोंके द्वारा सदासे किये जानेवाले कर्मोंको ही उन्हींकी तरह कर ॥
englishTranslation: Even seekers of freedom acted after knowing this. Therefore, do the work that your ancestors always did.
essence: Right action is the path even for those seeking freedom.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People seeking freedom acted in this way before you. So you should do the same rightful work that has long been done.
simpleInsight: You are not inventing the path alone. Steady action has always been the way through.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen before the war. Krishna reminds him that earlier seekers of freedom also understood this and still acted. The teaching pushes Arjuna back into his rightful duty.
modernRelevance: You are standing outside a hospital room, phone in hand, about to tell the truth no one wants. The path is not to escape the hard duty, but to do it cleanly.
translationLiteral: एवम् (thus) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / कृतम् (done) / कर्म (action) / पूर्वैः (by the earlier ones) / अपि (also) / मुमुक्षुभिः (by seekers of freedom) / कुरु (do) / कर्म (action) / एव (indeed) / तस्मात् (therefore) / त्वम् (you) / पूर्वैः (by the earlier ones) / पूर्वतरम् (from ancient times) / कृतम् (done) /
insight: It shifts the mind from hesitation to inherited responsibility: act because the work is right, not because it feels easy.
